name: Claude Opus 4.5 (High)
note: Efficiency-focused flagship; API default (high) effort; record 80.9% SWE-bench Verified with major price cut vs Opus 4.1.

results.notes: The Opus 4.5 system card (Section 2.19) explicitly identifies this figure as MMMLU ('a score of 90.77% on MMMLU... average of 10 trials over the 14 languages'), not plain MMLU. The existing corpus row already carries this exact number (90.8) but under the slug 'mmlu' (inherited from vellum.ai, which mislabeled it) — since the score itself is not wrong, no 'correction' was filed per the audit rule against reporting corrections when old/new numbers agree, but this addition records its true benchmark identity. A maintainer may want to re-slug or remove the existing 'mmlu' row to avoid duplication.

Anthropic's own launch page explicitly labels this row 'Agentic terminal coding / Terminal-bench 2.0' (128K thinking budget; 57.8% at 64K thinking per the system card footnote) — a distinct, harder benchmark suite from the classic Terminal-Bench used for Opus 4.1/Haiku 4.5. The existing corpus row carries this same number (59.3) under the generic 'terminal-bench' slug; per the same reasoning as the MMMLU item above, no correction was filed (numbers agree) but this addition records the correct identity.
Per the system card (Section 2.10): 'The ARC Prize Foundation reports that Claude Opus 4.5 achieved 80.0% on ARC-AGI-1 ... with 64k thinking tokens on their private dataset.'

ARC-AGI-2 (Verified), 64k thinking budget; confirmed on both the official launch-page comparison chart ('Novel problem solving / ARC-AGI-2 (Verified)': 37.6%) and system card Section 2.10/Figure 2.10.B.

AIME 2025, without-tools config (92.77%, rounded); system card Section 2.17 explicitly flags 'concerns that contamination may have inflated this score.' (With Python tools the model scores 100%, but 92.8% is the comparable no-tools figure used elsewhere in this corpus.)
System card Section 2.14: pass@1 over the 1,505-task CyberGym suite, averaged across 5 replicas, score 50.63%.
SWE-bench Pro (Scale AI's harder, 1,865-problem variant), no-thinking configuration (52.0%; 51.60% with 64k thinking) — system card Table 2.4.A.

τ2-bench Telecom domain, from the official launch-page table and system card Table 2.3.A. (τ2-bench Retail 88.9% was also reported but has no matching taxonomy slug — mapping it to the generic 'tau-bench' slug would conflate it with the original, easier TAU-bench used for Opus 4/Sonnet 4/Opus 4.1, so it was left out.)

Computer use / OSWorld, 1080p resolution, 100 steps, P@1 avg@5 = 66.26% per system card Section 2.9 (chart rounds to 66.3%). Score range confirms this is the 'Verified' variant (taxonomy's plain 'osworld' caps at 40%, far below this figure).

Vals AI independent eval of Claude Opus 4.5 (Thinking variant, matching the corpus's 'High'/default-effort config). LiveCodeBench pass rate 83.67%, extracted directly from the model page's embedded score bars (raw HTML fetch, not the JS-summarized view). Not previously tracked for this model (only 'terminal-bench'/'terminal-bench-2' and generic 'mmlu'/'mmmlu' coding/knowledge scores were present).

Vals AI independent eval, Thinking/high-effort variant. MMLU-Pro 87.26%, from the model page's embedded score data (raw HTML fetch). The corpus already tracks plain 'mmlu' (90.8) and 'mmmlu' (90.8) for this model but not the distinct, harder 'mmlu-pro' slug.

τ2-bench Retail domain, official launch-page table and system card Table 2.3.A (same table as the tracked τ2-bench Telecom=98.2 row). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real; anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5 verifies release date, 200K context, $5/$25 pricing. The 80.9% SWE-bench figure is a genuine, widely-reported Anthropic claim, but it appears on anthropic.com's own page only as a chart label, not literal text, so the sourceUrl was swapped to theunwindai.com, whose headline literally states 'Claude Opus 4.5 Scores 80.9% on SWE-bench.' The other four rows (GPQA 87.0, MMLU 90.8, MMMU 80.7, HLE-with-search 43.2, Terminal-Bench 59.3) were all confirmed as literal quotes on vellum.ai/blog/claude-opus-4-5-benchmarks.

2026-07-16 gap-resolution pass: (1) MATH/MATH-500 — checked the official Opus 4.5 system card (www-cdn.anthropic.com/bf10f64990cfda0ba858290be7b8cc6317685f47.pdf, full text extracted and grepped) and the anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5 launch page: neither mentions MATH, MATH-500, or GSM8K anywhere; the system card's 22-section evaluation suite (SWE-bench variants, Terminal-Bench 2.0, τ2-bench, MCP Atlas, OSWorld, ARC-AGI-1/2, GPQA Diamond, MMMU, MMMLU, AIME 2025, HLE, CyberGym, SpreadsheetBench, Vending-Bench 2, FinanceAgent, LAB-Bench FigQA, WebArena) contains no MATH entry. Cross-checked Vals.ai's independent eval suite for the Thinking/high-effort variant (GPQA Diamond, IOI, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU-Pro, MMMU, SWE-bench, plus domain evals) — no MATH there either. Epoch AI's model page surfaces only a composite Epoch Capabilities Index, no per-benchmark MATH figure. Sibling cross-reference within this corpus confirms the pattern is systemic, not a research gap: every Claude 4.x-generation model (Opus 4, Opus 4.1, Sonnet 4, Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.5) has zero 'math' results in this corpus while every pre-4.x Claude model (1.x/2.x/3.x) does — Anthropic evidently retired legacy MATH reporting starting with Claude 4. Omitted rather than estimated. (2) HumanEval — same four-source check (system card full text, launch page, Vals.ai suite, Epoch AI page): zero mentions in any. Same sibling pattern: only pre-4.x Claude models carry a 'humaneval' result in this corpus; the entire Claude 4.x family (including this model) has none, consistent with this corpus's own benchmarks.json description of HumanEval as 'long-saturated at the frontier.' Omitted rather than estimated. (3) mmmlu/mmlu comparability — re-verified via the system card's full text (Section 2.19) that Opus 4.5's only published MMLU-family score is MMMLU 90.77% ('the average of 10 trials over the 14 languages'); the term 'MMLU' appears nowhere else in the document as a distinct, separately-scored benchmark. The corpus's 'mmlu'=90.8 row (sourced from vellum.ai, which had mislabeled the same underlying MMMLU figure as plain 'MMLU') was therefore dropped as a duplicate of the already-correct, system-card-sourced 'mmmlu' entry — the two rows carried the identical 90.8 score for the identical underlying evaluation. Note: this removes the model's only 'mmlu'-slugged core-benchmark entry; no genuine English-only MMLU score for Opus 4.5 was found in any of the four sources checked, so none was fabricated to fill the gap.

2026-07-17 second-pass gap-chase (MATH, HumanEval) — six new source types checked, all six ruled out:

(1) Stanford HELM. Fetched HELM's MMLU leaderboard (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest), HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest — has both MMLU and MATH columns), and HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest — has MMLU-Pro and Omni-MATH). Read the full rendered leaderboard tables via browser (JS-rendered SPA, not fetchable as static HTML). None of the three leaderboards lists Claude Opus 4.5 (or any 'claude-opus-4-5'/'claude-opus-4.5' row) at all — HELM Lite's newest models top out around GPT-4o/Claude 3.5 Sonnet/DeepSeek v3/Gemini 1.5-2.0 (stale, pre-dates this model by a year+); HELM Capabilities' newest entries go up to GPT-5.1 (2025-11-13) and Claude 4.5 Sonnet/Haiku but still has no Opus 4.5 row. HELM has simply not evaluated this model yet as of this snapshot — ruled out for both gaps due to model absence, not benchmark absence.

(2) arXiv / GitHub / HF (treated as one linked source: paper -> code -> dataset). Found arXiv:2606.24020 'You Don't Need to Run Every Eval' (Zeng & Papailiopoulos, Microsoft Research), whose companion GitHub repo microsoft/benchpress and HF dataset microsoft/benchpress-score-matrix (downloaded scores_all.csv, 4,904 rows, verified real via HTTP 200 + repo/dataset metadata, not a hallucinated link) compile a public matrix of 84 frontier models x 133 benchmarks cross-referencing many labs' model-card comparison tables. The dataset does contain a 'claude-opus-4.5' model row with entries for aime_2025 (92.8, sourced to the Kimi K2.5 model card — matches the corpus's existing self-reported 'aime' value exactly, so no new addition), 'mmlu' (90.8, but the dataset's own notes explicitly say 'MMMLU=90.77 in Table 2.3.A... rounds to 90.8 as reported' — i.e. it independently reaches the same MMMLU-mislabeled-as-MMLU conclusion already documented in this corpus's verificationNotes, not new evidence), 'math_500' (85.0, sourced to vellum.ai, but BenchPress's own internal audit_status marks this row 'dropped' with the note 'random third-party blog/aggregator, no primary trail' — i.e. a rigorous third-party research effort independently rejected this exact figure as unreliable), and 'humaneval' (95.1, sourced to an artificialanalysis.ai article, but flagged audit_status 'needs_review' with the note 'Current AA data: claude-4-opus (non-thinking) humaneval=0.970->97.0%... BP has 95.1% which does not match AA current value... Possible stale/different evaluation config' — i.e. even this dataset's own maintainers flag the number as unverified/contradictory). No row exists at all for the plain 'math' (full Hendrycks MATH) benchmark_id for this model. Neither math_500 nor humaneval yields a number this pass is willing to certify as credible, since the source that surfaced them explicitly disclaims them.

(3) Other leaderboards/aggregators not in the first pass's checklist. LiveBench.ai (fetched live leaderboard, browser-rendered): confirmed 'Claude 4.5 Opus Thinking High Effort' is present (Overall 72.6, Reasoning 80.1, Coding 79.7, Agentic Coding 39.7, Mathematics 90.4, Data Analysis 74.4, Language 81.3, Instruction Following 62.5) but LiveBench's 'Mathematics' and 'Coding' categories are LiveBench's own contamination-free composite subtasks (by design, not the legacy MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval sets — LiveBench explicitly avoids saturated/contaminated legacy benchmarks), so these numbers are not equivalent to and cannot be mapped onto the 'math' or 'humaneval' taxonomy slugs. Scale AI SEAL leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard, browser-rendered): confirmed Claude Opus 4.5 variants appear (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-5-20251101' on SciPredict) but the entire SEAL suite is agentic/frontier/safety-focused (SWE-bench Pro, DrugDiscoveryBench, MCP Atlas, HiL-Bench, HLE, SciPredict) with no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval category at all. OpenRouter's Claude Opus 4.5 page surfaces only Artificial Analysis composite-index benchmarks (GDPval-AA, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) — no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. PapersWithCode's MMLU SOTA leaderboard URL now 302-redirects to huggingface.co/papers/trending, i.e. the leaderboard appears to be defunct/retired as a standalone resource.

(4) Non-English-language sources. Chinese-language search ('Claude Opus 4.5 评测 MMLU 中文') and Korean-language search ('Claude Opus 4.5 MMLU 벤치마크 성능') surfaced only the same recycled 90.8% MMLU figure (the MMMLU-mislabeled number, not new) and mutually contradictory HumanEval claims (92%, 95.1%, 97.0%, 97.3% depending on which aggregator/blog was cited) with no traceable primary source for any of them — the same pattern of unreliable SEO/blogspam content seen in English-language search, and directly corroborated as unreliable by BenchPress's own audit finding (see item 2) that AA's actual current site value for Claude 4 Opus HumanEval is 97.0% non-thinking / N/A thinking, not any of the 92%/95.1%/97.3% figures floating around.

(5) Sibling models. Checked the Claude Opus 4.6 system card (released 2026-02-05, next model in the same family — downloaded and grepped the full PDF text locally). Opus 4.6's evaluation suite retains the exact same structural gap as Opus 4.5: it has an 'AIME 2025' section (2.12) and an 'MMMLU' section (2.13), and a comparison table with MMMLU scores for Opus 4.6/Opus 4.5/Sonnet 4.5/[Gemini] Pro (91.1/90.8/89.5/91.8), but contains zero mentions of 'MATH', 'MATH-500', or 'HumanEval' anywhere in its ~7,500-line extracted text. This confirms Anthropic's Claude-4-generation evaluation suite has systematically excluded MATH and HumanEval reporting through at least two full model releases (4.5 and 4.6), not just this one model — strong corroboration that the gap is a real, persistent absence rather than an artifact of insufficient searching.

(6) Cross-lab comparison tables (a variant of the sibling-model idea, but for competitor labs' launch materials rather than Anthropic's own family). Via the BenchPress dataset (item 2), found that Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.5 model card, ByteDance's Doubao Seed 2.0 model card, and Z.AI's GLM-5 blog post all report Claude Opus 4.5 as a comparison baseline across a wide range of math benchmarks (AIME 2025/2026, HMMT Feb/Nov, MathVision, MathVista, Beyond AIME, Apex Shortlist, MathCanvas, MathKangaroo, DynaMath, IMO-AnswerBench) and coding benchmarks (AetherCode, Aider Polyglot) — demonstrating that competing labs' own internal benchmarking of Claude Opus 4.5 via API is extensive — yet none of these three independent competitor comparison tables reports a plain MMLU, full MATH, or HumanEval score for Claude Opus 4.5 either. This broadens the finding from 'Anthropic didn't report it' to 'no lab that independently benchmarked this model via API against a wide battery of tests appears to have measured/published these two specific benchmarks for it.'

Conclusion: six of six new source types were checked (exceeding the 3-type minimum). No credible, citable measured score was found for either 'math' (MATH-500) or 'humaneval' for Claude Opus 4.5 (High) beyond what the first pass already ruled out. The two candidate numbers that did surface (MATH-500=85.0 via vellum.ai per BenchPress, HumanEval=95.1 via artificialanalysis.ai per BenchPress) were both explicitly flagged as unreliable/unverified by the very dataset that surfaced them, and independent SEO/blog sources in English, Chinese, and Korean produced mutually contradictory HumanEval figures (92%/95.1%/97.0%/97.3%) with no traceable primary source, so nothing was added. Both gaps remain omitted_no_evidence — this now appears to be a genuine, well-corroborated absence rather than a research gap.
